prompt: Play this short three-step table-state action chain.

steps.prompt: Step 1: Hero is on the button and villain is in the big blind. Who is in position?
steps.feedback_correct_v1: Correct. The button acts later and keeps position on the big blind.
steps.feedback_incorrect_v1: Incorrect. The button is in position because hero acts after the big blind.
steps.why_v1: Position means acting later and seeing the other player act first.

steps.prompt: Step 2: Keep the same seats. Hero opened preflop and villain called. Who has initiative on the flop?
steps.feedback_correct_v1: Correct. Hero raised last preflop, so hero keeps initiative into the flop.
steps.feedback_incorrect_v1: Incorrect. The last aggressor carries initiative forward, and hero was the preflop raiser.
steps.why_v1: Initiative usually belongs to the player who made the last aggressive action.

steps.prompt: Step 3: Keep the same scene. The flop comes Jh Th 9c. Which simple action fits better on this pressure-building board?
steps.feedback_correct_v1: Correct. A coordinated two-tone flop supports the more pressure-building action.
steps.feedback_incorrect_v1: Incorrect. This dynamic flop supports a more pressure-building line than a passive call.
steps.why_v1: Connected two-tone boards create more pressure, so the more assertive action fits better.
